summary: Scoped acceptance reruns are green, but there is still a material false-green regression in the valuation-refresh lifecycle: while a refresh is running, the status endpoint serves a frozen in-memory payload instead of the latest persisted run state, so clients never see live progress or updated timestamps.

findings.title: Running valuation-refresh status is stuck at the start-time cache
findings.details: `/api/v1/stash/scan/valuations/status` returns `_PENDING_STASH_VALUATIONS` immediately whenever its `publishedScanId` matches the current published scan. That pending entry is seeded once at refresh start with zero progress and is not updated again until the background thread finishes, so polling clients cannot observe persisted `tabsProcessed/itemsProcessed` progress or fresher `updatedAt` values while the refresh is running. This is a task-scoped lifecycle regression, and the current tests miss it because they only cover start/dedup and published recovery paths, not a running refresh with advancing progress.
evidence: /home/hal9000/docker/codex_machine_foundation_release/data/environments/poe-goblin/worktrees/20260404-101114-a74c/poe_trade/api/app.py:557-573 seeds `_PENDING_STASH_VALUATIONS[scope]` once from the start payload, with zeroed progress, and there is no running-path mutation of that cache before completion.

Ad hoc repro in this worktree with `.venv/bin/python`: mocked `fetch_valuation_refresh_status_payload` to return `tabsProcessed=4` / `itemsProcessed=40`, but `GET /api/v1/stash/scan/valuations/status` still returned the cached pending payload with `tabsProcessed=0` / `itemsProcessed=0`.

/home/hal9000/docker/codex_machine_foundation_release/data/environments/poe-goblin/worktrees/20260404-101114-a74c/tests/unit/test_api_stash_valuations.py:1489-1500 only asserts the zero-progress start/dedup payload, while /home/hal9000/docker/codex_machine_foundation_release/data/environments/poe-goblin/worktrees/20260404-101114-a74c/tests/unit/test_api_stash_valuations.py:1239-1246 only covers published recovery; there is no coverage for a running refresh whose persisted progress advances.
